svg.title: Web Crawler Architecture Diagram
svg.desc: Interactive diagram showing the full web crawler workflow from Seed URLs through URL Frontier, HTML Fetcher, Parser, Duplicate Detection, Kafka, Modular Services, URL Filter, and URL Seen Detector looping back to the Frontier.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Seed URLs in a web crawler — what are they, how do you choose them, and give examples')
sendPrompt('Explain the URL Frontier in a web crawler in depth — priority queues, politeness queues, BFS, and how it decides what to crawl next')
sendPrompt('Explain the HTML Fetcher and Renderer in a web crawler — DNS resolution, caching, fetching content, and handling JavaScript-heavy pages')
sendPrompt('How does DNS resolution work in a web crawler and why do we cache DNS results?')
sendPrompt('What does the HTML Parser do in a web crawler? What does it parse, clean, and extract?')
sendPrompt('How does duplicate detection work in a web crawler? Explain exact hashing, SimHash for near-duplicates, and the trade-offs')
sendPrompt('Why do web crawlers use a content cache, and what technology is typically used?')
sendPrompt('What storage systems are used for web crawler content storage and why? Compare S3, Cassandra, and others')
sendPrompt('Why is Apache Kafka used in a web crawler architecture? Explain its role in decoupling services and ensuring durability')
sendPrompt('What does the analytics service do in a web crawler? What metrics does it track?')
sendPrompt('How does image downloading work as a modular service in a web crawler?')
sendPrompt('What does the URL Extractor do in a web crawler and how does it feed back into the URL Frontier?')
sendPrompt('What does the URL Filter do in a web crawler? List what it filters: robots.txt, blacklists, file extensions, etc.')
sendPrompt('How does the URL Seen Detector work in a web crawler? Explain Bloom Filters, their trade-offs, and how seen URLs are stored')
sendPrompt('What is URL Storage in a web crawler? What database is typically used and what metadata is stored?')

svg.text: Click any component to learn more
